assumptions: Audit was executed against the active git workspace because the requested OneDrive path does not exist in this environment.

Source of truth used: docs/architecture/Modultabelle.xlsx, docs/product/PRODUKT.md, docs/architecture/MAPPING.md, docs/planning/STATUS.md, docs/technical/SPECS_MVP.md, docs/_archive/2026-04-consolidation/*.
No standalone tracked 67-function equivalence matrix was found in the current repository.

parallel_or_duplicate_structures.note: Both /journey and legacy /trips are still routed.

parallel_or_duplicate_structures.parallel: none active, but old auth_and_identity paths remain in git history and docs

parallel_or_duplicate_structures.note: App-local files are now re-export shims, not separate models.
Generated artifacts make removed features look present.

verification.melos_non_interactive_note: dart run melos run test:* opens an interactive package-selection prompt in this terminal, so package-local test commands were used instead.

notable_doc_drift: Active docs still refer to melos.yaml although melos config is in pubspec.yaml.
apps/emma_app/README.md still describes the app folder as incomplete pre-flutter-create target.
trip_boundary_mapping_rules.md still contains pre-package migration wording.

improvements_since_last_known_state: Auth provider hoist is effectively completed: app-shell auth providers now live in apps/emma_app/lib/features/auth/presentation/providers/auth_providers.dart, while feature_auth provides data/widgets only.
Chat and directions are now behind ChatPort and DirectionsPort with Fake-First wiring and tests.
Journey engine/domain code was extracted out of app-local journey_engine stubs into packages/domains/domain_journey and packages/features/feature_journey.
A canonical /journey route and composition wrapper were added on top of feature_journey.

New active workspace packages since the 2026-04-16 state: adapter_gemini, fake_chat, fake_maps, domain_customer_service, domain_employer_mobility, domain_reporting, feature_auth, feature_employer_mobility.
Test coverage increased materially across auth, home chat, journey widgets/domain services, fakes, employer mobility, and UI kit.
regressions_since_last_known_state: Tracked app-local feature trees for mobility_guarantee, partnerhub, and migration were deleted after 2026-04-16 without visible tracked replacements.
Documentation still references removed feature paths and outdated workspace files, creating a false sense of feature availability.
Legacy /trips remains routed in parallel to the newer /journey flow, keeping duplicate routing surfaces alive.
Several implementations now look integration-ready by naming (RemoteDataSource, Service, Adapter) while remaining hardcoded mock/placeholder code.
critical_functional_gaps: No explicit ticketing domain/port/product catalog exists.
Subscriptions/D-Ticket remain placeholder-level and are split across subscriptions and employer job-ticket mocks.
Check-in/check-out is a placeholder screen only.
Notifications are a placeholder screen only and no NotificationPort exists.
Realtime monitoring and operational journey guidance are not implemented as real user-trip monitoring.
Partnerhub, sharing, on-demand, taxi, and partner transactions are not implemented beyond textual or model-level hints.
No standalone tracked 67-function equivalence matrix is available for row-level parity sign-off.
architecture_conflicts: melos.yaml is absent, but multiple active docs still treat it as canonical.
JourneyCase still describes itself as a 5-phase journey while JourneyPhase and the module table define 8 phases.
TariffPort in code only exposes getAvailableTariffs(), contradicting SPECS_MVP M11.
Money/value models across tariff/subscription/payment/budget/job ticket still use double instead of cent-safe integer amounts.
Provider-only-in-app-shell target is only partially implemented: auth is hoisted, feature_journey and feature_employer_mobility still define providers in packages.
Legacy /trips and canonical /journey remain active side by side.
Docs still reference tracked mobility_guarantee/partnerhub/migration feature paths that no longer exist in the repository.

mock_solutions_that_look_real.reason: Service name suggests a real TRIAS backend, but returns hardcoded locations/trips after Future.delayed.
Real adapter class exists but throws UnimplementedError for calculateRoute.
RemoteDataSource name suggests real HTTP transport, but returns hardcoded routes and locations.
RemoteDataSource backed by Dio, but all outputs are mock hardcodes.
RemoteDataSource backed by Dio, but all outputs are mock hardcodes.
RemoteDataSource backed by Dio, but all outputs are mock hardcodes.
RemoteDataSource returns demo users; no real IdP call happens.
Repository name suggests tariff backend integration, but exposes a local mock D-ticket and a distance formula.
RemoteDataSource is mostly TODO/placeholder with UnimplementedError.

gaps: No deep intent handoff into booking/ticketing phases
No proactive end-to-end mobility orchestration
No multilingual assistant behaviour beyond generic text handling
traceability_14_modules.next_step: Bind the home assistant directly to JourneyCase orchestration instead of only prompt enrichment.

gaps: No consent-management module
No account deletion or data export path
No globally enforced multi-profile logic
No payment method / wallet ownership
traceability_14_modules.next_step: Split identity, account, and consent into explicit flows and add missing compliance/account use cases.

gaps: No real calendar/location/weather/realtime source is wired
Current user-facing flow still relies on heuristics and hardcoded context
traceability_14_modules.next_step: Wire DemandRecognitionEngine to explicit fake/real context providers and surface its result in the app.

gaps: Real routing data is missing
Partner modes are modeled but not implemented
Legacy /trips and canonical /journey run in parallel
traceability_14_modules.next_step: Finish the migration to a single canonical /journey planning flow.

gaps: Code contract contradicts SPECS_MVP M11
Only mock tariff data is exposed
No product catalog or quote API
Money model is not cent-safe
traceability_14_modules.next_step: Align TariffPort and tariff models with SPECS_MVP M11 and add dedicated tests.

gaps: No dedicated ticketing domain or port
No QR/barcode ticket artifact
No end-user reachable commit/rollback flow
traceability_14_modules.next_step: Introduce domain_ticketing/TicketingPort and connect Tickets/Payments screens to real ticket state objects.

gaps: End-user routing to the full JourneyScreen is still missing
Repository still builds a static demo case instead of persisted live cases
traceability_14_modules.next_step: Route the app into JourneyScreen and bind JourneyCase to real session/user data.

gaps: No RealtimePort or event stream
No actual monitoring of booked user trips
No operational notification path
traceability_14_modules.next_step: Define RealtimePort and a minimum fake realtime event source for live journey monitoring.

services_ports: Guarantee flags inside Journey fulfillment control
gaps: No tracked domain_mobility_guarantee package
No claim submission/status UI
Docs and generated artifacts imply a feature that is not tracked
traceability_14_modules.next_step: Decide whether guarantee is intentionally removed or needs a real tracked package/feature.

gaps: No partner registry/onboarding/SLA/monitoring logic
Tracked app-local partnerhub feature was removed
traceability_14_modules.next_step: Either define a minimal canonical partnerhub scope or de-scope the docs.

gaps: Wallet/payment are modeled but not operational
Subscriptions and job tickets are split instead of unified
Profile mode does not govern all downstream transactions
traceability_14_modules.next_step: Unify subscription/job-ticket/payment/wallet ownership across identity, employer mobility, and ticketing.

gaps: No CRM backend or escalation workflow
No SLA/ownership split between emma and partners
Screen is not routed
traceability_14_modules.next_step: Anchor support cases in a real service flow instead of only exposing them from the demo JourneyCase.

gaps: No persistent learning loop
No transparent optimization controls
No consent-governed analytics feedback path
traceability_14_modules.next_step: Either explicitly de-scope learning for MVP or implement a minimal persisted routine/preferences update loop.

gaps: No reporting sink or KPI dashboard
No SLA reporting or audit-trail UI
Screen is not routed
traceability_14_modules.next_step: Define minimum operating KPIs/events and wire them to a real reporting sink.

equivalence_check.gap: Demo/placeholder-level parity only; not yet equivalent to production-grade existing apps.
No real realtime adapter or operational journey monitoring.
No real purchase flow, wallet, or ticket artifact.
No unified subscription or D-ticket lifecycle.
No domain, no UI flow, no booking.
No real sharing integration or booking.
Structurally strong, but still backed by mock data and not globally enforced across transactions.
Cases are displayed, not operationally processed.
Receipt draft exists, but no PSP, persistence, or operational checkout.

recommended_next_10_steps: Bring the missing 67-function equivalence matrix into the repository or bind the audit to a concrete external source.
Update active docs to the current workspace reality and remove melos.yaml references.
Decide whether mobility_guarantee, partnerhub, and migration were intentionally removed or must return as tracked features.
Retire or explicitly quarantine the legacy /trips flow in favor of the canonical /journey flow.
Raise TariffPort and tariff models to the SPECS_MVP M11 contract with cent-safe money types.
Introduce an explicit ticketing domain/port and a real ticket artifact model.
Unify subscriptions, D-ticket, employer job tickets, payments, and wallet ownership.
Define RealtimePort and NotificationPort with a minimal fake-first implementation.
Resolve the provider-location rule by hoisting remaining feature package providers or updating the rule.
Add real integration tests for the end-to-end user journey instead of keeping integration_test empty.
blockers_open_decisions: The requested OneDrive repo path is missing locally; the audit is based on the active git workspace.
No standalone tracked 67-function equivalence matrix is present.
The authoritative hierarchy between the 14-module table, 16-module product catalog, 18-domain model, and STATUS.md is not fully settled when they conflict.
It is unclear whether deleted mobility_guarantee/partnerhub/migration features were intentionally de-scoped or accidentally lost from tracked code.
The roadmap for real adapters in routing, tariff, realtime, partner transactions, and notifications is still undecided.
